briefing.headline: Pitch it tight — post-spawn clear water puts a jig in its best element.
briefing.gameplan:
In post-spawn with clear water and stable conditions, jigs presentations shine.

Johnston: Use a compact mini flipping jig instead of full-size. You catch more fish and get more bites. With ever-increasing fishing pressure, a smaller jig is what it takes to get bit.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Johnston: Use a compact mini flipping jig instead of full-size. You catch more fish and get more bites. With ever-increasing fishing pressure, a smaller jig is what it takes to get bit.
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, dock, point. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Keep baits close to the bottom on ledges, but often burn them and kill it to trigger a strike — that retrieve variation produced key fish
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, dock, point. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: Post-spawn offshore on Table Rock, the 3 key elements were brushpiles, isolated cedar trees, and bluff end points/big long main-lake points where schools related to current and hard bottom — mixed species (smallmouth, largemouth, spotted ...

Hackney: 'In the South, shad spawn on hard bottoms, matted vegetation, cypress trees, and boat docks. Old Styrofoam marina docks are the best. On the Tennessee River, the better shad spawn is on shell beds and shallow bars in 5-6 feet of water.'

KVD: 'Shad spawn happens around 72 degrees, after bass/crappie spawn — Mother Nature helping them recover by providing a feast. Spikes on full moon, lasts 2-4 weeks. Starts in one area of lake — keep moving until you see activity. Most happens at ...

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable post-spawn conditions split fish between shallow fry-guarders (2-5ft) and transitioning females (5-15ft). Shad spawn activity on seawalls, riprap, and bluff walls concentrates bigger fish shallow at dawn.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize lures and switch to brighter colors
If bites are subtle, try a lighter jig weight — slower fall rate can be the difference
